First one is, what tool do you use to measure LLM visibility?
And second, we've talked a lot about AI, but what's the balance that you strike between human intervention and AI within the content creation process?
Yeah, that's a great question.

Okay, so on the LLM visibility piece, we're using an API right now through Scrunch, which is a partner, but we're actually off the record building our own, because it's all, what we're finding is just like, you're just paying a premium and they're all paying the same APIs.
And what we found is even for the customers that we're measuring, like the most important thing is like the context you feed it into these projects and identifying the right personas.
And so if you go into any of these tools out there that measure visibility, the way they do a company description.
The way they identify your personas and the way they come up with topics to measure, and even the way those questions are awarded are horrible.
I don't know if you've seen differently, but from our experience, and we work with a lot of customers, and so that's what we're really good at.
Like pinging an API and perplexity and saying, hey, return the citations here and tell me if this brand shows up, is more of just kind of like a simple thing to do.
And so that's why we first wanted to deliver value to customers quickly.

So we partnered with Scrunch to use their API, but then we're not only looking at that, like we're also pulling your GSC and also your analytics and correlating all three because you do need to understand like kind of the engagement on the page, how you're showing up with search and things like that.
You know, so we kind of merge all those and we're every week delivering that as well.
So like we're in Slack with you and interacting, right?
And then on the last piece, like the balance really depends.
So I'll give an example.
We have a customer call.
have customer call.
We Thank
So Biologica, they're actually still in pre-launch.
It's the guy that started Allbirds.
And for them, it's like the quality has to be like so, so high because their chief medical officer reviews everything, you know.
And so I'll show you like kind of just the example here.
But so what we do is like we just take a lot, a different process in the calibration, right?
So for instance, with their, let me use this one as an example.
So what we've done is built a custom of a few custom steps in addition to fact-checking.
We also try to reverse engineer the medical doctor.
And so for instance, in this one, what it does is like the doctor is a holistic doctor with a background in endocrinology.
And so what we did is like figure out, like based on a couple rounds of reviews, these are the comments, her name's Maggie, that Maggie's making.
And so then it's like we take each one of these and run kind of a review step into each one of these.
And so that allows us to do it's just like almost like LLM is a judge with a building more and more, but not right from the beginning.
So for instance, like this one reviews any mention of ingredients because ingredients are really important.
They're a supplement essentially for women in different age groups, right?
Like anytime their brand is mentioned, annotate a medical claim so that if there's any medical claims here, like it will annotate it.
And so like what we try to do is not go from zero to quote unquote automated, right?
What we're doing is trying to understand.
And obviously it's like the quality bar because of the technical complexity and, you know, in what space it is, is even higher.
Then we'll just reduce the volume a little bit and go a lot deeper and treat it more like a thought leadership.
Right.
And so it really depends on the bar and the calibration, but, and then what we're trying to do is over time, like reverse engineer that so that it can scale as well.
Right.
Does that, does that help a little bit?
I, I know that's a little different.
Excuse me.
I had a question too.
So I, I understand the content production process, which is all, yeah, very cool how you do all that.
But taking a step back, how do we work with you to really define the strategy and like how we're going to move forward?
Cause it seems like there's endless possibilities.
So how do we kind of like hone in on what we could do together?
Yeah.
Great, great question.
So that's actually one of the reasons in the last three months we've changed our approach to do what we call a strategy and execution sprint.
So it's eight weeks and it's just a fixed cost.
And the benefit of that is that like first week, we're going super deep interviewing you all, uh, ingesting anything you send us by week two.
Then we're starting to map out.
Both like the context that we're creating, the starting calibration on content quality, but also start to map out the different topic clusters that we want to go after based on your audiences.
Then by week three, hopefully we're aligning on that and finishing calibration.
And week four, hopefully like we're starting to execute, right?
So for instance, like with Engine, we are now week nine, I believe, or week 10, and we've done 47 pieces of content and the majority of them net new.
But we also have an entire content OS that has like over 500 assignments flagged already that we came up with from our different audits and just a different topic clusters.
We identify 16 topic clusters, and then we prioritize a few of them.

And then every week we're doing a mix of net new and refresh across different content clusters and then monitoring, you know, and this is where like obviously like your expertise and your insight into the business.
Like, hey, there wasora.
It's a new product we're launching.
So for instance, with Ramp, before they launch accounts payables, and they did the whole Super Bowl ad for four weeks straight, that's all we were focusing on from a content perspective.

And so that kind of helps, where you essentially have this team that can both go heads down, focus on one lane because the business needs it, if it's a new lane, or we can kind of take almost like a portfolio strategy, and you can kind of help guide us as far as like, what, how should we prioritize the different opportunities we flag for you, you know?
Does that answer your question a little bit?
Yeah, yeah, thank you.
Yeah, yeah.
Well, I know we don't have a ton of time, but as you can tell, like, we're insanely, insanely excited to potentially work together.
And the way we've done the strategy sprint is really de-risking it.
And so at the end of the eight weeks, you can fire us.
We can fire you is kind of how I joke.
And so, like, the burden is really...

Like on us to set the right strategy, skill quality, and kind of show you like what we can, we can do, you know, and, and how we collaborate and, and then give you the confidence from there to go into kind of the execution mode, although we're already executing a ton in that first eight weeks as, as well.
This is great.
Thank you very much, Marcel and Tyler.
So I think as a team, we could kind of regroup and see how everybody thinks about it.
Is it something worth our time to give it a try?
And then if so, then Marcel, I could kind of regroup and see what the next steps kind of looks like.
That sounds perfect.
Well, thank, thank you all team.
And thanks for joining early and late.
I appreciate it.
We'll, we'll hopefully talk soon.
Okay.
Thank you very much.
Take care.
Take care.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore potential partnership between GrowthX and Navan for AI-powered content marketing and SEO optimization.](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=5.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX offers an end-to-end AI-powered content creation and SEO optimization service, combining agency expertise with a proprietary platform](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=374.0)
  - [Their approach focuses on high-quality, well-researched content that performs well in both traditional search and AI-powered search results](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [GrowthX proposes an 8-week strategy and execution sprint to demonstrate capabilities and set initial strategy before longer-term engagement](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1569.0)

## Topics

### Navan's Content Marketing Challenges

  - [Small team managing content for both PLG and SLG, needing to scale content production](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=272.0)
  - [Desire to improve SEO rankings and visibility in AI search results](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
  - [Need to balance editorial bandwidth with content production demands](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=330.0)

### GrowthX's AI-Powered Content Creation Process

  - [Uses AI workflows for research, drafting, and fact-checking](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1132.0)
  - [Incorporates human editors for quality control and brand alignment](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1237.0)
  - [Creates detailed "artifacts" (company context, writing guidelines, personas) to inform AI content generation](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1053.0)
  - [Produces highly-cited, well-researched content that performs well in AI search results](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1611.0)

### Content Refresh and Optimization

  - [Monitors content performance (traffic, engagement, quality) to identify refresh candidates](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1719.0)
  - [Uses both AI analysis and manual selection to prioritize content for updates](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1751.0)
  - [Can handle complex content types (e.g., calculators, programmatic pages) with custom workflows](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=1975.0)

### LLM Visibility Tracking

  - [Currently uses Scrunch API for tracking, but developing in-house solution](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2182.0)
  - [Correlates data from Google Search Console, analytics, and AI visibility metrics](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2244.0)
  - [Delivers weekly reports and insights via Slack](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2269.0)

### Strategy Development and Execution

  - [8-week strategy and execution sprint to align on goals, map topic clusters, and begin content production](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2439.0)
  - [Flexible approach to balance ongoing content needs with specific campaign support](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2502.0)
  - [Collaborative process to prioritize content opportunities based on business needs](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2532.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Navan team to internally discuss potential engagement with GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2605.0)
  - [If interested, Nima to follow up with Marcel on next steps for potential partnership](https://fathom.video/share/NCW6EB-GhVLXHh4eu4-nZw5vbDvVAeYw?tab=summary&timestamp=2620.0)

action_items.description: Regroup with Navan team to discuss GrowthX proposal, evaluate if worth pursuing 8-week strategy sprint
If team agrees to proceed, follow up with Marcel re next steps for GrowthX engagement
